text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, with a strong undercurrent of "AI bubble" fears. This directly impacts your heavily concentrated tech/chip portfolio, especially the leveraged names. 

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.95%) and SPAL (-37.57%) are your main concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning they decay over time and amplify drops, which hurts extra on margin. Respect their stops. 

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further news about AI market fatigue or sector-wide pullbacks. Your stops on SOXL and SPAL are critical. 

Bottom line: Risk management on your leveraged, underwater positions is paramount in this uncertain tech environment.
plays: SOXL: Respect the stop at 200. Given the "SELL" status and leveraged decay, consider trimming if it approaches that level.
SPAL: This is a new, leveraged position down -37.57%. The default stop at 21.38 is important; consider tightening it given the volatility and margin.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The market sentiment for tech/chips is too cautious to initiate new long positions right now.
